title: Fix text visibility with blue background

requirements.text: Change the main app background color to blue. Update the CSS custom properties in frontend/src/index.css to set the background (--background) to a solid blue color (e.g., a mid-range blue like #1E40AF or similar). Ensure this applies to the overall page/app shell.

quotes: again the text are invisible change the background as blue

acceptanceCriteria: The main application background is visibly blue
The blue background is consistent across all sections of the app

requirements.text: Ensure all text throughout the application is clearly visible against the blue background. Audit and update text color CSS custom properties (--foreground, --muted-foreground, --card-foreground, etc.) in frontend/src/index.css and any inline styles in components (App.tsx, WelcomeScreen.tsx, ResumeForm.tsx, ResumePreview.tsx, and all section components) to use high-contrast colors (white or very light shades) so no text is invisible or hard to read on the blue background.
acceptanceCriteria: All heading, body, label, placeholder, and button text is clearly readable against the blue background
No text disappears or blends into the background in any section of the app
Form field text and labels inside the resume form are fully visible
The resume preview section maintains its own legible text styling

constraints: Do not edit any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
The resume preview (ResumePreview.tsx) should retain a clean, print-friendly appearance with dark text on a white/light preview card background — only the outer app shell background should be blue
nonGoals: Changing the resume preview card background color
Modifying backend logic or data schema
